subtopics.title: Introduction to Algebra
subtopics.teaching_note: Algebra uses letters to represent unknown numbers. Variable: letter that can take different values (x, y, n). Constant: fixed number (3, 5, -2). Coefficient: number multiplying a variable (in 3x, 3 is the coefficient). Expression: combination of terms (3x + 2y - 5). Simplifying: combine like terms (terms with same variable). Example: 3x + 2y + 5x - y = 8x + y. Worked examples: If x = 3, find 2x + 5. 2(3) + 5 = 11.

subtopics.title: Solving Linear Equations
common_misconceptions: You can add different terms together
Moving a term across = doesn't change sign
2x means x + x (true, but 2x also means 2 × x)
subtopics.teaching_note: Equation: mathematical sentence with = sign. Golden rule: Do the same to both sides. Steps: 1) Simplify both sides. 2) Collect variable terms on one side, constants on the other. 3) Divide by coefficient. Worked example: 2x + 5 = 13. 2x = 13 - 5. 2x = 8. x = 4. Check: 2(4) + 5 = 13 ✓. Word problems: Translate words to equations. 'Three times a number plus 7 equals 22' → 3x + 7 = 22 → x = 5.

subtopics.title: Angles and Lines
concepts: sum of angles on straight line
common_misconceptions: Parallel lines never meet (in Euclidean geometry)
Alternate angles are always equal (only for parallel lines)
All angles that look equal are equal
subtopics.teaching_note: Key facts: Angles on a straight line sum to 180°. Angles at a point sum to 360°. Vertically opposite angles are equal. Parallel lines cut by transversal: Alternate angles are equal (Z-shape), Corresponding angles are equal (F-shape), Interior angles sum to 180° (C-shape/U-shape). Types of angles: acute (< 90°), right (= 90°), obtuse (90° < angle < 180°), reflex (> 180°). Complementary: sum to 90°. Supplementary: sum to 180°.
